findings.description: Pure math computation module — no SQL, no file I/O, no LLM calls, no network access. Zero attack surface for injection or data exfiltration.

NaN safety: compute_brier_score() returns float('nan') for empty verified sets. track_calibration() guards with math.isnan() check at lines 106-107, defaulting to 0.5. ECE returns None for <10 verified predictions — correctly handled as nullable float in CalibrationResult.

Division safety: all divisions are guarded — len(verified) == 0 checked before compute_brier_score/compute_direction_accuracy, len(preds) > 0 checked before bucket rate calculation (line 86), len(verified) >= 10 checked before ECE computation (line 53).

Store interaction: duck-typed store parameter — only calls store.update_calibration(tracker_id, dict). No direct SQL execution. The store's own security properties (parameterized queries in learning_store.py) provide the SQL injection defense.

notes: Low-risk pure-computation module. All division operations have zero-guards. NaN is explicitly handled. The duck-typed store parameter means this module works with any store implementing update_calibration(tracker_id, dict) — not coupled to LearningStore specifically. No security concerns.
